exercises.exerciseTypeName: A: Complete using the verbs in the box
exercises.title: A: Complete using the verbs in the box
exercises.instructions: Use the verbs in the box: apologise, defend, introduce, recognise, rent, respect, trust.
exercises.studentInstruction: Use the verbs in the box: apologise, defend, introduce, recognise, rent, respect, trust.

items.sentence:
'Who's that woman near the door?'
'That's Olivia Grant, the writer. Let me ...... you.'

'Hello, Max!'
'Wow, Emma! I didn't ...... you with those glasses!'

'I'm sorry I forgot your birthday.'
'It's okay. You don't need to ...... .'

'We're moving to the city centre next month.'
'Are you going to buy or ...... a flat there?'
items.explanation: Pay to use a home → **rent** a flat.

items.sentence:
'I told Jake my secret and now I'm worried.'
'Relax. You can ...... Jake. He's very loyal.'

'Mrs Lewis volunteers at the animal shelter every weekend.'
'I really ...... people like her.'
items.explanation: Admire her volunteering → **respect** people like her.

items.sentence:
'I don't think Mia broke the window.'
'Why do you always ...... Mia when something happens?'

exercises.exerciseTypeName: B: Write one word in each gap
exercises.title: B: Write one word in each gap
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Can you look ...... my dog while I'm visiting my grandparents?
items.explanation: Take care of → look **after** my dog.

items.sentence: Rachel and Ben split ...... a few weeks ago.
Liam and Noah have fallen ...... because of a football match.
Sophie was brought ...... by her grandmother.
items.explanation: Raised as a child → brought **up** by grandmother.

items.sentence: Is Chloe really going ...... with Ethan?
items.explanation: In a relationship → going **out** with Ethan.

exercises.title: C: Word formation
exercises.instructions: Complete by changing the form of the word in capitals.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: I have a lot of ...... for my music teacher. (ADMIRE)
Jake is so ...... — he always forgets his keys. (CARE)
Nobody believed Oliver because he's such a ...... . (LIE)
Many ...... athletes compete at international level. (ABLE)
Ella said sorry, so I ...... her straight away. (FORGIVE)
My cousin has a funny and friendly ...... . (PERSON)
I don't have enough ...... to speak in front of everyone. (CONFIDENT)
People who are ...... often lose their friends' trust. (HONEST)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The context points to "was built" — hundreds of years ago.

explanationContent.usefulTip: When you see a finished past-time expression, reach for the past simple first.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: A finished past time marker (yesterday, last week, ago) requires the past simple, not a perfect tense.

exercises.title: E: Complete the sentences
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: We've known each other ...... we were children.
items.explanation: From childhood → known each other **since** we were children.

items.sentence: Daniel gets ...... really well with his neighbours.
My grandparents have been ...... for more than fifty years.
Sofia has a good ...... with all her classmates.
Mark is very ...... of his little brother and always helps him.
My cousins grew ...... in a small village near the coast.
items.explanation: Spent childhood → grew **up** in a village.

items.sentence: Emily is an only ......, so she sometimes feels lonely.
Jake and his grandfather are very ...... and spend lots of time together.
They fell ...... after arguing about money.
After the argument, she felt really ...... .
Mia got ...... with her best friend again after saying sorry.
items.explanation: Friends again → got **back** with her friend.

items.sentence: My neighbour ...... after our cat while we were away.
items.explanation: Took care of → **looked** after our cat.

items.sentence: Lucas and I have a lot ...... common because we both love basketball.
She ...... him her best friend because they tell each other everything.
items.explanation: Best friend → **calls** him her best friend.

items.sentence: You can always ...... on your real friends when you need help.
items.explanation: Rely on → **count** on your real friends.
